title: Example Project - RAG Chatbot for Google Reviews
text:
Google reviews are often noisy, redundant, and unstructured. Businesses need a fast way to understand what people are saying without reading thousands of comments.



I created a local chatbot using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that allows a user to ask natural language questions about a business and get instant answers based on the review corpus.

Clustering reviews with BERTopic
Indexing with FAISS
Using LLaMA 3.2 for natural language generation
Built with FastAPI and served via a simple interface





Fast and relevant responses to complex user queries
High user satisfaction in internal tests
Potential for real-world deployment for businesses with large review volumes




Python, FastAPI, LLaMA 3.2, BERTopic, SentenceTransformers, FAISS, Selenium, BeautifulSoup, React (WIP)




GitHub Repository (placeholder link)
Demo: Coming Soon

title: Welcome
text:
Hi, I’m Mohamed El Ghali
I’m a data scientist with a strong math background. I work mostly on NLP and machine learning projects — from topic modeling and sentiment analysis to building local RAG pipelines with LLMs.
I like solving real problems with clean, understandable tools. Sometimes that means digging into theory, other times it means scraping messy data and making it usable.

I believe good ideas deserve solid execution — even if that starts small.


🔬 View Projects 📄 About Me 📬 Contact

title: About Me
text: I’m Mohamed El Ghali Benabou, a data scientist and applied mathematician with a deep interest in natural language processing, machine learning, and real-world applications of AI, especially in domains like ESG analytics, interactive AI systems, and intelligent search interfaces.

title: About Me
text:
Academic Background

Master’s in Statistics and Econometrics, Mohammed V University, Rabat (2021–2023)
Bachelor’s in Mathematics, Jean-François Champollion University, Albi, France (2014–2015)
Preparatory Classes for Engineering Schools – Salmane Al-Farissi, Morocco

title: About Me
text:
Practical Experience

Final Project Intern at AIOX Labs – ToumAI Analytics

Built a fine-tuned LLM to assess ESG impact of African companies
Scraped and processed multilingual data from media & social platforms
Applied BERTopic, sentiment analysis, and visualization pipelines

Intern at the Ministry of Maritime Fisheries – HR department
(Earlier observational internship)

title: About Me
text:
Personal Projects

RAG Chatbot for Google Reviews (2024 - Ongoing)
Local NLP assistant for review summarization using BERTopic + LLaMA 3.2 + FastAPI
NOVAI – Novel/News Scraping + Recommender (2024 - Ongoing)
Full-stack app with RAG + LangChain for custom search and reading experience, with TTS, keyword interaction, and smart suggestions
Titanic ML Competition
Exploratory classification project using Random Forest to identify survival factors

You can find these projects and others on my GitHub.

title: About Me
text:
Academic Projects

Unsupervised Learning Algorithms – PCA, Clustering (R)
SARIMA vs ML for Time Series Forecasting
Life Expectancy vs Development Status
Multivariate Studies with PCA, CA, MCA (SPSS)

Read the reports here.

text:
Skills
Languages & Tools: Python, R, Scikit-Learn, Pandas, NumPy, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, FastAPI, LangChain, SQL, Streamlit, PowerBI, SPSS, Git, Docker
Modeling & Theory: BERT, BERTopic, RAG, LSTM, CNN, HDBSCAN, CA/MCA, Econometrics
Web: FastAPI, Flask, aiohttp, BeautifulSoup, React (in progress)

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
Friend is a voice-enabled chatbot built as an experimental tool — or, as the name implies, a small “friend” you can talk to. It runs either using local LLMs or connects to Groq API if no local model is available.
The goal wasn’t to solve a business problem, but to test how far you can go combining speech input, language models, and TTS output with minimal tooling.

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
Friend is a voice-enabled chatbot built as an experimental tool — or, as the name implies, a small “friend” you can talk to. It runs either using local LLMs or connects to Groq API if no local model is available.
The goal wasn’t to solve a business problem, but to test how far you can go combining speech input, language models, and TTS output with minimal tooling.

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
How It Works
There are two ways to run the app: - A Jupyter Notebook interface - A Python CLI version

Under the hood:



Friend Chatbot Workflow



A while loop waits for user input
If no speech is detected after 2 seconds, it:

Records audio
Converts it to text
Logs the input into a simple text-based history

Then:

If a local LLM is available, it runs the input there
If that fails, it sends the input to Groq API (external LLM)

Once a response is received:

The answer is added to the history
It’s spoken back to the user using TTS
The app then waits for the next message




UX Detail
This is an experimental tool — built to test, learn, and better understand what it means to talk to machines in a more natural way. It wasn’t designed to scale, but to serve as a proof of concept for private, voice-based interaction with language models. It’s simple, and it works.

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
Tech Stack

Python (CLI + Notebook)
TTS + Speech Recognition
LLMs (Groq API, Local Model fallback — Ollama)
File-based logging and history

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
Notes

“The most interesting part was realizing how simple this is to build with the right tools. It’s not fancy — but it works. And it was fun to make.”

title: Friend – A Private Voice Chatbot
text:
Code

GitHub Repository – Friend


This is an experimental tool — built to test, learn, and better understand what it means to talk to machines in a more natural way. It also serves as a proof of concept for private, voice-based interaction with language models. It’s simple, and it works.
